text: fat is 14.7g. the fat quality is doing a lot of the work here. for a fruit, sugar moderation is the whole game.
carbs is 8.5g. the fibre support is doing a lot of the work. for fruit, the real question is whether the sweetness stays under control.
protein is 2g. protein quality is a real strength here. for a fruit, you want sweetness to be kept in check.
vitamin c at 11% DV, vitamin a at 2% DV stand out most. that is barely moving the needle.
minerals are basically not part of the case here.

on the plus side. a true outlier for fruit fat quality because this behaves nothing like typical sweet fruit and that makes the category result exceptional. easy to use as a whole food because simple, recognisable, and realistic for everyday eating. brings a clean fruit identity because it still contributes something valuable even when it is not elite.

the drawbacks next. calorie density is much higher than most fruits because still useful, but it changes how casually you can throw it in. sugar can climb fast because a lot of fruits look healthy but still lean sweet. satiety is not especially strong because fruit often needs help from the rest of the meal.
A tier. Best when you want sweetness that still earns its place nutritionally.
